name: Tim Hecker
bio: Tim Hecker builds dense ambient records that keep earning through specialist streaming, sync-adjacent use, and deep catalog listening rather than mainstream hit volume.
notable_for: Immersive ambient composition, critical acclaim, and durable specialist-audience engagement.
career_highlight: Albums like Virgins and Ravedeath, 1972 turned abstract sound design into a durable long-tail catalog.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes creator-leaning rights participation, strong specialist streaming depth, and modest but durable licensing-style value.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split between label-side distribution and creator-affiliated control
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing appears concentrated around the composer side
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No major catalog sale adjustment is assumed here
ownership.notes: Experimental ambient catalogs tend to monetize depth and long-term specialist use rather than mainstream scale.
